name: azure-communication-sms-java
description: Send SMS messages with Azure Communication Services SMS Java SDK. Use when implementing SMS notifications, alerts, OTP delivery, bulk messaging, or delivery reports.
capabilities: Send SMS to single recipient with delivery confirmation
Send SMS to multiple recipients in batch
Configure delivery reports via Azure Event Grid
Support async operations for high-throughput scenarios
Handle error responses and HTTP status codes
Use AzureKeyCredential or connection string authentication
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Azure Communication Services SMS Java SDK documentation skill. The static analyzer flagged 40 potential issues, but after evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES: backticks are markdown code block syntax (not shell commands), URLs are placeholder examples in documentation, and code references are standard error handling patterns. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Send SMS messages from Java applications using Azure Communication Services. Supports single messages, bulk sending, delivery reports, and OTP notifications.
userTitle: Send SMS with Azure Communication Services
limitations: Phone numbers must be in E.164 format
Rate limits apply based on Azure subscription tier
Delivery reports require Event Grid configuration

useCases.title: Two-Factor Authentication
useCases.description: Send OTP codes for user verification and account security

useCases.title: Transaction Notifications
useCases.description: Send order confirmations, shipping updates, and payment alerts

useCases.title: Marketing Campaigns
useCases.description: Send bulk promotional messages to customer segments

promptTemplates.title: Basic SMS Send
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the azure-communication-sms-java skill to send a verification code '+14255551234' from my Azure ACS number
promptTemplates.scenario: Send a simple SMS message to one recipient

promptTemplates.title: Bulk SMS with Options
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the azure-communication-sms-java skill to send a flash sale notification to multiple recipients with delivery reporting enabled
promptTemplates.scenario: Send SMS to multiple recipients with custom options

promptTemplates.title: Async Bulk Send
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the azure-communication-sms-java skill to send async bulk messages to a list of phone numbers with tagging for tracking

promptTemplates.title: Error Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the azure-communication-sms-java skill to show how to handle rate limiting errors and invalid phone numbers when sending SMS
promptTemplates.scenario: Implement proper error handling for SMS operations

bestPractices: Always use E.164 phone number format (+[country code][number])
Enable delivery reports for critical messages like OTPs
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for 429 errors
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode connection strings in source code - use environment variables
Do not send SMS synchronously in high-traffic APIs without queuing
Do not skip error handling - individual recipients can fail while others succeed

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: Three methods: DefaultAzureCredential (recommended), AzureKeyCredential, and connection string.

faq.question: What phone number format is required?
faq.answer: E.164 format is required, for example +14255550100.

faq.question: How do I enable delivery reports?
faq.answer: Set deliveryReportEnabled=true in SmsSendOptions and configure an Event Grid subscription.

faq.question: What happens if a message fails for one recipient?
faq.answer: Other recipients still receive their messages. Check isSuccessful() for each result.

faq.question: Is there rate limiting?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure applies rate limits based on your subscription. Handle 429 responses with retry.

faq.question: Can I send to international numbers?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must enable toll-free messaging in your Azure subscription and use E.164 format.
